rationale: The citation is used in the methods development, not just in a background list. The paper explicitly states that its two-stage 2sCOPE procedure is similar to the control-function approach of Petrin and Train (2010) because it adds a first-stage residual-type term to correct endogeneity. It then contrasts its contribution against that approach by emphasizing that 2sCOPE is IV-free, so Petrin and Train serves as a meaningful methodological benchmark for positioning the new estimator.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the control-function logic exemplified by Petrin and Train (2010) and adapts it into a copula-based two-stage generated-regressor method. It refines that setup by removing the need for IV-based exclusion restrictions in constructing the endogeneity correction term.
